description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly white upper surface and dark gray to black lower regions. The stone features a rough, fractured texture with sharp edges and angular faces, suggesting it was broken from a larger mass rather than smoothed by weathering. Subtle beige and brown inclusions are visible, adding to its natural, variegated appearance. The surface is mostly matte with some slightly smoother patches, and the color transitions are abrupt rather than gradual. There are no visible carvings or artificial modifications, and the stone appears dense and solid throughout. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural obstacle or decorative element in urban landscapes such as parks or sidewalks.
description: An irregular, rough stone with a white top and dark gray-black base, featuring sharp edges and natural color variations. Its fractured texture and solid appearance make it a prominent urban obstacle or decorative element.
description_view_0: The stone's white upper surface is most prominent, with dark gray and black areas visible along the lower edge and a few beige inclusions.
description_view_1: This view highlights the angular transition between the white and dark regions, with a pronounced, jagged edge on the right side.
description_view_2: The stone appears more elongated, with the dark gray and black areas dominating and the white surface receding to the background.
description_view_3: A mix of white and dark surfaces is visible, with the stone's rough, fractured texture and sharp corners most apparent.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and abrupt color transitions between white, dark gray, and black, with some small beige inclusions; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Freshly fractured with sharp edges and rough surfaces, showing minimal signs of long-term weathering or smoothing.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts sharply with paved or manicured urban surfaces, but can blend naturally with other stones or landscaping elements when placed among them.
